Title: MsbZIP55 regulates salinity tolerance by modulating melatonin biosynthesis in alfalfa

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acetylates serotonin to form N-acetylserotonin in melatonin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant and free radical scavenger that enhances salinity tolerance by improving ROS detoxification and ion homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in all tested tissues; highest in mature leaves and stems; induced by salt, peaking at 6 h after 100 mM NaCl
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in all tested tissues; highest in mature leaves and stems
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Mainly chloroplast; also detected in nucleus and cytoplasm
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 100 mM NaCl short-term induction; 300 mM NaCl for salt-tolerance phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression; RNAi construct for knockdown
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq under 100 mM NaCl for 3 h (NCBI GEO/related accession: PRJNA1184504); metabolite profiling by UPLC-MS/MS for melatonin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in alfalfa; RNAi knockdown in alfalfa; RT-qPCR; subcellular localization; melatonin quantification by UPLC-MS/MS; salt-stress phenotyping; ROS, antioxidant enzyme, and ion-content assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes melatonin accumulation and enhances salt tolerance; reduces Na+/K+ ratio and oxidative damage under salt stress
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MsSNAT1 is the alfalfa SNAT1 homolog and is induced by salt stress. 2. MsSNAT1 is mainly chloroplast-localized and acts as a rate-limiting melatonin biosynthesis enzyme. 3. Overexpression increases endogenous melatonin, antioxidant enzyme activities, and ion homeostasis, improving salinity tolerance. 4. RNAi knockdown lowers melatonin and causes salt-sensitive phenotypes. 5. MsSNAT1 is directly repressed by MsbZIP55.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering salt-tolerant alfalfa by constitutive or stress-inducible upregulation; can be combined with regulators that relieve repression such as MsbZIP55 editing/silencing; monitor possible pleiotropic effects on growth under field saline conditions
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious morphological difference under normal growth conditions was reported for overexpression lines
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper identifies MsSNAT1 as the decisive melatonin-biosynthetic enzyme experimentally linked to salinity tolerance in alfalfa; downstream antioxidant and ion-homeostasis genes measured include MsCAT, MsPOD, MsCu/Zn-SOD, MsSOS1, and MsNHX1

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: basic leucine zipper (bZIP) transcription factor; closest homolog of AtbZIP55
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Salt-induced; transcript peaks at 3 h after 100 mM NaCl treatment
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Salt-induced; transcript peaks at 3 h after 100 mM NaCl treatment
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional repression via promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant and free radical scavenger that supports salinity tolerance through ROS detoxification and ion homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 100 mM NaCl for induction and RNA-seq; 300 mM NaCl for phenotyping; exogenous 100 μM melatonin rescue treatment
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression and GFP fusion; RNAi construct for knockdown
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq under 100 mM NaCl for 3 h identified candidate TFs (accession: PRJNA1184504); melatonin profiling by UPLC-MS/MS
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome screening; dual-luciferase assay; GUS reporter assay; alfalfa overexpression; RNAi knockdown; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; melatonin measurement; exogenous melatonin rescue; salt-stress physiological assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces melatonin accumulation and salt tolerance by repressing MsSNAT1; overexpression causes salt sensitivity, while RNAi enhances salt tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MsbZIP55 was identified from salt-responsive transcriptome data as a regulator of MsSNAT1. 2. MsbZIP55 directly binds the MsSNAT1 promoter in vitro and in vivo and represses its transcription. 3. Overexpression lowers MsSNAT1 expression and melatonin content, causing higher ROS damage and Na+/K+ imbalance under salt stress. 4. RNAi knockdown elevates MsSNAT1 expression and melatonin accumulation, improving salt tolerance. 5. Exogenous melatonin partially/completely alleviates the salt-sensitive phenotype of MsbZIP55 overexpression lines, supporting that MsbZIP55 acts through melatonin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising negative regulator target for gene editing or silencing to increase endogenous melatonin and salt tolerance in alfalfa; could be combined with MsSNAT1 activation; caution that bZIP TFs may have broader stress-network functions
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression increases salt sensitivity, oxidative damage, and Na+ accumulation under salinity; no obvious phenotype under normal conditions was reported
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although MsbZIP55 transcripts are induced by salt, its function is negative for salt tolerance, suggesting possible additional post-transcriptional or protein-level regulation not resolved in this study
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct upstream repressor of the rate-limiting melatonin biosynthesis gene MsSNAT1 and therefore a decisive negative controller of melatonin accumulation under salt stress
